fault_hypothesis: The condition on line 60 is inverted. Clamping is applied when `clamp` is `False` instead of when it is `True`.

constraints.intent: If clamping is enabled, the result can only be correct if no extrapolation occurred (i.e., t is in [0,1]), because the clamping logic is erroneously skipped.
When entering this branch (clamp is True), the result is only correct if no clamping was needed in the first place.

spec.expr: not clamp or ((y0 <= y1 and __cbfl_result >= y0 and __cbfl_result <= y1) or (y1 < y0 and __cbfl_result >= y1 and __cbfl_result <= y0))

constraints.intent: The final return value must be within the vertical range of the segment [y0, y1] if clamping is enabled.
An unclamped, extrapolated value (t outside [0,1]) is invalid if clamping was requested.
